# Markdown to PDF - Comprehensive Example

Welcome to the ditto converter! This document demonstrates all currently supported features.

## Text Formatting

This converter supports **bold text**, *italic text*, and ***bold and italic*** text.

You can also use ~~strikethrough~~ text.

## Lists

### Unordered Lists

- First item
- Second item
  - Nested item A
  - Nested item B
- Third item

### Ordered Lists

1. First step
2. Second step
3. Third step

### Task Lists

- [ ] Task to do
- [x] Completed task
- [ ] Another task

## Blockquotes

> This is a blockquote.
> It can span multiple lines.
>
> You can have multiple paragraphs.

## Code

### Inline Code

Here's some `inline code` in the middle of a sentence.

### Code Blocks

```rust
fn main() {
    println!("Hello, world!");
}
```

```python
def greet(name):
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

print(greet("World"))
```

```javascript
function greet(name) {
    return `Hello, ${name}!`;
}
```

## Headings

# Heading 1 (H1)
## Heading 2 (H2)
### Heading 3 (H3)
#### Heading 4 (H4)
##### Heading 5 (H5)
###### Heading 6 (H6)

This markdown converter is being actively developed. Stay tuned for more features!
